name: Henbane Witches
description: Henbane (Hyoscyamus niger) holds a prominent, often sinister, position in European folklore and witchcraft traditions. Revered and feared for its potent psychoactive properties, henbane was a key ingredient in witches' flying ointments and potions. Its use dates back to ancient times, with evidence suggesting its presence in rituals and medicinal practices. Henbane contains tropane alkaloids like hyoscyamine, scopolamine, and atropine, which can induce hallucinations, delirium, and a sense of detachment from the body, contributing to the sensation of flight reported by witches. In folklore, henbane is linked to the dark arts, necromancy, and communication with spirits. It was believed to open gateways to other realms and facilitate astral projection. The plant's association with witches stems from its ability to induce altered states of consciousness, which were interpreted as spiritual journeys or encounters with supernatural entities. Henbane was also used in love spells and divinatory practices, although its toxicity made it a dangerous and unpredictable tool. Its use required extensive knowledge and caution, further solidifying its connection with skilled practitioners of magic. The plant's dark reputation is reinforced by its physical characteristics; its foul odor and sticky leaves contribute to its aura of danger and mystique. Henbane's cultural importance lies in its representation of the blurred lines between medicine, magic, and the supernatural in European history.
subtitle: Herb of witches and altered states.

keyMyths.title: Witches' Flying Ointment
keyMyths.description: Henbane was a key ingredient in ointments that witches applied to their skin, allowing them to experience the sensation of flight and astral travel. The alkaloids in henbane induced hallucinations and a detached state, perceived as spiritual journeys.
keyMyths.source: Various historical accounts of witchcraft trials and folklore

sources.author: Nicholas Culpeper
sources.description: A 17th-century herbal providing information on the medicinal and astrological properties of herbs, including henbane.
sources.text: A 17th-century herbal providing information on the medicinal and astrological properties of herbs, including henbane.

sources.author: Carlo Ginzburg
sources.description: Examines the historical and anthropological evidence for the use of psychoactive substances in witchcraft rituals.
sources.text: Examines the historical and anthropological evidence for the use of psychoactive substances in witchcraft rituals.
